AIML Engineering Lab1 / 11
The Problem

1:160 Imbalance: 99.4% accuracy predicts nothing

Left: 49,686 pass vs 314 fail in a batch (1:158). Right: Label noise with 42 Pass-as-Fail and 17 Fail-as-Pass noisy labels. Standard cross-entropy predicts "pass" for every chip: 99.4% accuracy, 0% defect recall.

Why FocalLoss, not SMOTE?

FocalLoss (α=0.75, γ=2.0) adds a modulating factor (1-p)γ to cross-entropy loss. Easy majority samples get near-zero loss weight, forcing the model to focus gradient updates on missed defective chips. SMOTE creates synthetic minority samples by interpolation, but for sensor data like cell leakage and retention time, interpolated values create physically impossible feature combinations. FocalLoss modifies the loss function directly without generating fake data.

DRAM Test Features

FeatureWhat it measuresWhy it matters
test_temp_cChip temperature during testHot spots = process defects
cell_leakage_faCell current leak (femtoamps)High leak = retention failure
retention_time_msCharge hold time (ms)Below spec = bit errors
gate_oxide_thickness_aOxide layer (Ångstroms)Too thin = breakdown
vt_shift_mvThreshold voltage shift (mV)Off-target = unreliable
trcd_nsRow-to-column delay (ns)Slow access = timing fail
AIML Engineering Lab2 / 11
A100 GPU Training

HybridTransformerCNN on
NVIDIA A100-SXM4-40GB

Top left: Loss convergence over 50 epochs with bfloat16. Top center: AUC-PR per epoch. Top right: Precision-recall curves for val/test/unseen splits (near-identical = no overfitting). Bottom: Confusion matrices. 3,374 true defects caught out of 15,595 total. 34,075 good chips flagged for retest (acceptable: retest costs $0.10/chip vs $1000+ field failure).

0.816
AUC-ROC
0.054
AUC-PR (primary metric)
50
Epochs (best: 48)

Why AUC-PR, not AUC-ROC? At 1:160 imbalance, AUC-ROC inflates from massive true negatives. We catch 21.6% of defects but AUC-ROC shows 0.816. AUC-PR ignores true negatives and honestly reflects minority class detection. Our 0.054 is 8.6x better than random (0.00625). A100-SXM4-40GB is the same GPU on Colab Pro, server-grade NVLink socket with 40GB HBM2e memory.

AIML Engineering Lab3 / 11
Critical Engineering Insight

The float16 Death Spiral
that killed 4 training runs

float16 + GradScaler
FocalLoss: (1-p)^gamma
# 5-bit exp overflows
GradScaler / 2 / 2 / 2
scale to 0, loss collapses
bfloat16, no GradScaler
float165-bit exp = +/-65,504
bfloat168-bit exp = +/-3.4x10^38
bfloat16 has float32 exponent range.
Focal gradients never overflow.

The fix: Changed torch.float16 to torch.bfloat16. Removed GradScaler. AUC-PR recovered from 0.001 (collapsed) to 0.054, a 54x improvement. Hardware-aware numerical analysis for production training.

4
Runs killed by float16
54x
AUC-PR recovery
bfloat16
8-bit exponent fix

Why loss goes high, stays, then drops: Early epochs: model is random (high loss). Middle: learns easy majority first; FocalLoss then forces focus on hard minority (temporary loss bump). Late: cosine LR schedule stabilizes convergence.

AIML Engineering Lab4 / 11
40-Day PSI Drift Heatmap

Drift escalated for 13 days.
Then the retrain gate opened.

What is PSI? Population Stability Index measures distribution shift per feature per day. PSI < 0.1 = stable, 0.1-0.2 = warning, > 0.2 = critical. Calculated as ∑(P_i - Q_i) × ln(P_i/Q_i) comparing production bins against training baseline. If any critical feature exceeds threshold, drift alarm fires.

  • D1-8
    Baseline period. No drift. PSI near zero across all 6 features. Model performing at validation benchmarks.
  • D9
    False alarm: retention_time_ms PSI spiked to 1.47 from a one-time batch anomaly (equipment calibration). Staleness gate prevented unnecessary retraining. Day 10 auto-recovered.
  • D11-29
    Gradual drift escalated across features. PSI exceeded 5.0 by Day 29. Retrain blocked by staleness: model was <30 days old. AUC-PR degrading steadily.
  • D30
    RETRAIN TRIGGERED. All 3 gates opened: PSI 5.25, AUC-PR dropped 8%, model 30 days old. 50 epochs on full day's data, bfloat16 on A100.
  • D31-35
    Post-retrain recovery. New v2 model outperformed v1. AUC-PR improved above baseline.
  • D39
    CANARY FAILED. Bad model deployed to 10% canary pod. AUC-PR crashed. Automatic rollback to v2 within seconds.
  • D40
    System recovered. Rolled-back v2 model stable. Pipeline proved self-healing without human intervention.
AIML Engineering Lab5 / 11
Retrain Logic

3-gate retrain trigger.
All 3 must be TRUE.

Gate 1: Statistical Drift (PSI)

Any critical feature PSI > 0.2 for 3+ consecutive days. Prevents reaction to single-day noise. retention_time_ms was the primary drift signal.

Threshold: PSI > 0.2, sustained 3+ days
Gate 2: Performance Degradation

>5% AUC-PR drop vs baseline. Compares current batch inference against the champion model's validation benchmark.

Threshold: 5% relative AUC-PR degradation
Gate 3: Model Staleness

>30 days since last retrain. Prevents reactive retraining from transient drift spikes that resolve on their own.

Threshold: 30 calendar days since last training
Result: Day 30 Retrain

All 3 gates opened simultaneously on Day 30. PSI at 5.25, AUC-PR dropped 8%, model was 30 days old. Fully automated retraining fired.

All 3 gates must be TRUE to retrain
Why 3 gates, not 1?

PSI alone fires on normal batch variance. Performance alone is noisy at 1:160 imbalance. Staleness alone wastes compute. Combined: precision retraining.

Low-Data Drift Tagging

When batch <10K rows, PSI is statistically unreliable due to sparse bin counts. Drift is tagged in MLflow but never triggers retraining until data volume is sufficient.

AIML Engineering Lab6 / 11
Production Infrastructure

6 services. One command.
Fully orchestrated.

Apache Airflow

3 DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs): daily_inference, retrain_trigger, master orchestrator. Each DAG defines task dependencies without cycles. SLA monitoring alerts if any task exceeds its time budget. Automatic retry with exponential backoff.

Apache Kafka

Streaming DRAM test records from fab sensors. Consumer group routes records to FastAPI inference engine in real time. Handles 5M+ messages/day with partitioned topics for parallel processing.

Apache Spark

Batch ETL on 5M-row daily slices. 4.7x faster than pandas at this row count. Reads raw sensor data, applies feature engineering, writes parquet to S3. Total: 8.5GB parquet across 40 production days.

MLflow + Registry

Tracks every experiment run including the 4 failed float16 runs. Model Registry maintains champion/challenger versions. Automatic promotion on canary success, rollback on failure.

Prometheus + Grafana

Prometheus scrapes /metrics from FastAPI every 15 seconds. Grafana dashboards show per-feature PSI, inference latency (p50/p95/p99), request rates, prediction distribution. Alerts on SLA breach.

FastAPI + K8s

REST API with Pydantic validation, Redis prediction cache (5-min TTL). K8s with HPA (2-8 pods), canary deployment (90/10 traffic split). 120ms p99 latency under load testing.

To run locally: cd deploy/docker && docker compose up -d starts all 6 services. Airflow :8080, MLflow :5001, Grafana :3000, FastAPI :8000/docs.

AIML Engineering Lab7 / 11
System Architecture

5-Layer MLOps:
Data, Train, Serve, Monitor, Retrain

Architecture: Data layer ingests 16M+ fab sensor records. Training layer runs HybridTransformerCNN with FocalLoss on A100 GPU. Serving layer delivers predictions via FastAPI in Docker/K8s. Monitoring layer tracks drift with PSI and model metrics via Prometheus/Grafana. Retrain loop fires only when all 3 gates are satisfied.

Production Monitoring Dashboard

Grafana shows: Request rate, latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99), prediction distribution (pass/fail ratio), CPU usage with HPA threshold, error rate, and pod autoscaling. All scraped by Prometheus every 15 seconds.

AIML Engineering Lab8 / 11
Hardware Benchmark

CPU training = 69 hours.
A100 = 1.6 hours. GPU is not optional.

69 hr
CPU (Apple M3)
3.5 hr
T4 (16GB)
1.6 hr
A100 (40GB)

Wall clock time = actual elapsed time on your watch. The A100 processed 16M rows in 50 epochs in 1.6 hours of real time. The full 40-day production run (200M total rows, all drift checks, 1 retrain, canary evaluation, rollback logic) completed in 219 minutes wall clock on A100. Each "simulation day" represents one real production day in a DRAM fab, compressed into 5.5 minutes of compute.

Why HybridTransformerCNN on tabular data?

1D-CNN captures local patterns between adjacent features (e.g., leakage + retention combined signal). Transformer self-attention captures global feature interactions across all 6 features simultaneously. Per-feature tokenization gives each sensor its own learned embedding. This architecture consistently outperforms pure ML baselines on high-dimensional sensor data.

Data scale

16M training rows, 8.5GB total parquet across 40 production days (5M rows/day). All stored on S3 as compressed columnar parquet. Spark ETL handles daily batch processing. The pipeline is chip-agnostic: swap DRAM features for NAND flash or CPU wafer data, same architecture works.

AIML Engineering Lab9 / 11
40-Day Production Run

Full system response to drift:
detect, retrain, evaluate, rollback

40
Production days
200M
Total rows
219 min
Wall clock
1
Rollback

How to read this: 40 simulation days map to 40 real production days (Feb 20 to Mar 31, 2026). Each day processes 5M rows of DRAM sensor data. "Wall clock" = 219 minutes of actual GPU compute time to simulate the entire 40-day run. The system autonomously handled stable operation, gradual drift, a false alarm, triggered retraining, caught a bad model via canary testing, and auto-rolled back. Zero human intervention at any point.
